title: Umami Rich Irish Lamb Stew
description: A fermented and velvety Irish lamb stew featuring shoyu-koji and bhuna, perfect for a comforting meal on a sick day, made in a traditional tajine clay vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together shoyu-koji, bhuna spice blend, and a pinch of salt.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the shoyu-koji mixture.
Heat a couple of tablespoons of oil in the tajine clay vessel over medium heat.
Brown the lamb cubes in batches until browned on all sides, then set aside.
Add more oil if necessary, then saute the chopped onion until it is softened and translucent.
Add the chopped carrots and potatoes to the vessel, cooking for a few minutes until they start to soften.
Add the browned lamb, lamb broth, and tomato paste to the vessel, stirring to combine.
Cover the vessel with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the stew for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the vegetables are cooked through.
